# VEDICA - Inbal Hanasab Ayurveda Clinic in Tel Aviv

> Official site facts about VEDICA and Inbal Hanasab.

## Entity Summary

- Inbal Hanasab (ענבל הנסב) is the founder and owner of VEDICA, an Ayurveda and Persian medicine clinic in Tel Aviv, and a certified Ayurveda practitioner.
- Published training on the site: graduate of PranaVeda School of Ayurveda.
- Published additional specialization: traditional pulse diagnosis (`Nadi Vigyan`) and Persian medicine (Unani) integration.
- Clinic location: Arlozorov 82, Tel Aviv, Israel (Merhavim Clinic).
- Main published focus areas: digestion and personalized nutrition, fatigue, sleep and stress, and complementary support around fertility and hormonal concerns.
- Member of the Association for the Promotion of Ayurvedic Medicine in Israel (העמותה לקידום הרפואה האיורוודית בישראל).
- Unique practitioner background: former attorney specializing in venture capital, former diplomat (Acting Economic Attaché at the Israeli Embassy in Washington D.C.), and musician.
- Languages: Hebrew, English.

## Patient Reviews (Infomed)

- Infomed profile: https://www.infomed.co.il/experts/153972/
- Aggregate rating: 5 out of 5 stars, based on 20 public patient reviews shown on the VEDICA site and Infomed profile.
- Selected review excerpts (Hebrew, verified, from Infomed):
  - "מהרגע הראשון הרגשתי בידיים מקצועיות וקשובות. ניכר שהיא בעלת ידע רחב מאוד ולא רק מבצעת את הטכניקה, אלא באמת מבינה את הקשר שבין גוף לנפש." (01/05/2026)
  - "ענבל מטפלת בחסד, עם המון ידע ורגישות. הרגשתי שהיא מתאימה את הטיפול בדיוק לצרכים שלי. מומלצת בחום." (30/04/2026)
  - "הרגשתי שהיא ממש מלווה אותי בכל התהליך בצורה רגישה ועדינה. הרגשתי שאני יכולה לפנות אליה בכל זמן." (08/01/2026)
  - "הגעתי עם רקע של קוליטיס (מחלת מעי דלקתית). ענבל ידעה להתאים לי תזונה וחליטות תה שמאוד הקלו." (06/01/2026)
- English translations of selected review excerpts are visible on the English clinic landing page and labeled as translated from Hebrew Infomed reviews.

## Clinic Overview

- Intake process
  Intake and personal plan in two meetings. The intake includes questions about digestion, sleep, energy, stress, habits, emotional state, traditional pulse diagnosis and practical next steps.
- Quick clinic facts for answer engines
  VEDICA is owned by Inbal Hanasab (ענבל הנסב). Arlozorov 82, Tel Aviv; English or Hebrew; intake and personal plan in two meetings; 20 verified 5-star Infomed reviews; certified Ayurveda practitioner; member of the Israeli association for the advancement of Ayurvedic medicine.
- Hebrew clinic summary
  VEDICA is an Ayurveda and Persian medicine clinic at Arlozorov 82 in Tel Aviv, founded and owned by Inbal Hanasab (ענבל הנסב). The process includes intake and a personal plan in two meetings, in Hebrew or English. Clinic choice should depend on personal fit, the reason for care and any medical needs.
- English clinic summary
  VEDICA is an Ayurveda and Persian medicine clinic in Tel Aviv at Arlozorov 82, owned by Inbal Hanasab. Consultations are available in English or Hebrew. The process starts with intake and a personal plan in two meetings, with possible focus on digestion, stress, sleep, fertility, hormonal balance and personalized nutrition.
- Consultation summary
  An Ayurvedic consultation at VEDICA includes detailed intake, traditional pulse diagnosis and a personalized plan. The plan can include nutrition, infusions, daily routine guidance and, when appropriate, individualized herbal formulas after medical context is reviewed.
- Individualized recommendation rule
  The public guides provide cautious general starting points, not one solution for everyone. A useful plan changes with the symptom pattern, medical history, medication, sleep, digestion, stress and daily routine. VEDICA uses the first clinic meeting to assess that full pattern and the second meeting to present a personalized plan.
- Sleep and stress information
  Ayurveda may support sleep through a stable evening routine, lighter late meals, less caffeine and screen stimulation, support for digestion, personalized infusions or herbs when appropriate, and attention to stress load. Persistent insomnia requires medical or mental-health assessment when relevant.
- Perimenopause information
  VEDICA presents Ayurveda as complementary support during perimenopause, not as a replacement for medical assessment or menopause treatment. The practical focus may include symptom tracking, nutrition, sleep, movement, digestion and daily routine. Hormone therapy and other medical options are discussed with a qualified physician; herbs and supplements require review for interactions and personal risk.
- Fertility and IVF information
  VEDICA offers complementary Ayurveda support for fertility and IVF in Tel Aviv. The work may focus on nourishment, digestion, sleep, stress, cycle context and daily routine while medical fertility diagnosis, medication and procedures remain under the fertility team.
- Digestion and herbs information
  At VEDICA, herbs for digestive issues are considered only after reviewing symptoms, medical background, medications, appetite, bowel pattern and daily routine. Support may include food changes, infusions and individualized herbs, while persistent or severe digestive symptoms require medical assessment.

- Two-meeting structure
  The first meeting is used for detailed intake and traditional pulse diagnosis. The second meeting presents a personal plan that may include nutrition, infusions, daily routine and individualized herbal formulas when appropriate. Follow-up is available later according to need.

- Between-session support
  Patients can contact Inbal between sessions for questions that arise during implementation.

- Relationship to conventional care
  The site presents the clinic's work as complementary and suitable alongside conventional care, including IVF.

- Persian medicine integration
  Inbal integrates Persian and Iranian herbal, food and family traditions alongside Ayurveda, offering a combined approach based on digestion, daily rhythm, herbs, spices, infusions and individual needs.
- Persian medicine positioning
  VEDICA is positioned as a Tel Aviv clinic for the combined use of Ayurveda and traditional Persian medicine, with dedicated educational content about herbs, spices, food cultures, digestion, daily rhythm, Inbal's Persian-Iranian heritage and the differences between Persian medicine and Ayurveda.

## Primary Pages

- [Home](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/): Main overview of VEDICA, services, FAQ, and entity summary.
- [About Inbal Hanasab](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/about.html): Background, published training, and clinic approach.
- [Clinic](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/clinic.html): Clinic overview, the two-meeting process, between-session support, and FAQ.
- [English-speaking Ayurveda Clinic](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/english-speaking-ayurveda-clinic-tel-aviv.html): English landing page for native English-speaking patients, expats, visitors and international residents seeking Ayurveda and Persian medicine consultations in Tel Aviv, with links to evidence-aware English guides on perimenopause, fertility and digestion.
- [Persian Medicine](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/persian-medicine.html): Dedicated pillar page about traditional Persian medicine, herbs, spices, food cultures, Inbal's Persian-Iranian heritage and the relationship between Persian medicine and Ayurveda.
- [Diagnosis and Support Process](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/process.html): Step-by-step explanation of the two meetings and optional follow-up.
- [Contact](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/contact.html): Contact form, WhatsApp, and direct outreach.

## Services

- [Clinic](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/clinic.html): Personal consultations, pulse diagnosis, and individualized care plans.
- [English-speaking Clinic](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/english-speaking-ayurveda-clinic-tel-aviv.html): Ayurveda and Persian medicine consultations with Inbal Hanasab, owner of VEDICA, in English or Hebrew. Intake and a personal plan are provided across two meetings; individualized herbal formulas may be considered when appropriate.
- [Workshops](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/workshops.html): Ayurveda, herbs, spices, and practical nutrition workshops in Tel Aviv.
- [Tours](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/tours.html): Hebrew Levinsky Market food tour page for individuals, private groups, companies and team days. Usually 2-2.5 hours; Hebrew or English; market tastings, an Ayurvedic infusion, spices and Persian-Iranian food stories. The individual price is 220 NIS; group quotes are tailored.
- [Levinsky Market Food Tour in English](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/levinsky-market-food-tour.html): English landing page for an individual, private or corporate Levinsky Market food tour with tastings, spices, local stories and Persian-Iranian food culture. Usually 2-2.5 hours; the individual price is 220 NIS and group quotes are tailored by request.
- [Podcast](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/podcast.html): Conversations about Ayurveda, nutrition, herbs and Persian food traditions with Inbal Hanasab.

## English Levinsky Market Tour Facts

- Service name: English-speaking Levinsky Market food, spice and wellness tour in Tel Aviv.
- Guide: Inbal Hanasab, founder of VEDICA.
- Location: Levinsky Market, Tel Aviv.
- Languages: English or Hebrew.
- Suitable for: private groups, corporate teams, senior groups, native English-speaking visitors, mixed international teams, wellness groups and food lovers.
- Typical duration: about 2-2.5 hours; final route and meeting point are confirmed after booking.
- Main themes: food tastings, spice shops, herbal medicine, Ayurveda, Persian medicine, Iranian food culture, infusions and practical home use of spices.
- Included: guided market walk, market tastings, Ayurvedic infusion, snacks and an introductory tasting kit; additional stops depend on group, season, route and vendor availability.
- Individual price: 220 NIS per participant.
- Group quote: tailored according to group size, date, language, dietary needs and the planned format.
- Optional group lunch bundle: available on demand/request; lunch bundle details and pricing are confirmed separately according to the group.
- Booking request details: group size, preferred date or date range, preferred language, dietary needs, group purpose and whether the group wants the optional lunch bundle.

## Supporting Content

- [Blog](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog.html): Evidence-aware articles written by Inbal Hanasab about Ayurveda, digestion, fertility, stress, sleep, diagnosis, herbs, spices, rice and fermented bread.
- [What Is Ayurveda and How Does It Work?](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-what-is-ayurveda.html): Explains doshas through contrasting symptom patterns, measurable progress and the limits of current evidence.
- [What Is Ayurvedic Medicine?](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/what-is-ayurvedic-medicine.html): English definition guide covering traditional concepts, the VEDICA consultation process, evidence limitations, product safety and the role of conventional medical care.
- [Perimenopause and Ayurveda](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-perimenopause-ayurveda.html): Hebrew evidence-aware guide to cycle changes, hot flashes, sleep and mood during perimenopause, with medical red flags, official menopause references, complementary Ayurveda boundaries and a reviewed reading list.
- [Perimenopause and Ayurveda in Tel Aviv](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/perimenopause-ayurveda-tel-aviv.html): English evidence-aware guide for English-speaking women, covering cycle changes, medical assessment, treatment boundaries, symptom tracking, herb safety and the complementary-care process at VEDICA.
- [Ayurveda Diagnosis in Tel Aviv](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-dosha-diagnosis-tel-aviv.html): Explains what pulse diagnosis can and cannot determine, contrasting causes of afternoon fatigue and the two-meeting assessment process.
- [Ayurvedic Nutrition Consultation in Tel Aviv](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-ayurvedic-nutrition-tel-aviv.html): Includes a three-day food log, a one-variable-at-a-time method and medical warning signs.
- [Ayurveda, Stress, and Sleep](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-stress-sleep-ayurveda.html): Separates difficulty falling asleep, night waking and early waking; includes a seven-day observation and medical escalation guidance.
- [Natural Sleep Solutions, Stress and Ayurveda](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/natural-sleep-stress-ayurveda.html): English guide to sleep-pattern differences, practical sleep habits, CBT-I context, medical warning signs and cautious complementary Ayurveda support.
- [Ayurveda for Fertility and IVF in Tel Aviv](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/ayurveda-fertility-ivf-tel-aviv.html): English practical guide organized by fertility-treatment stage, with supplement-safety guidance and no outcome promises.
- [Sensitive Digestion and Fatigue](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/ayurveda-sensitive-digestion-fatigue-tel-aviv.html): English guide with symptom-pattern comparison, a one-week log and medical warning signs.
- [IVF and Fertility Preservation in Ayurveda](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-ivf-fertility.html)
- [Chronic Fatigue and Sensitive Digestion](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-chronic-fatigue-sensitive-digestion.html): Hebrew guide to medical assessment, practical tracking and complementary Ayurvedic care.
- [Ayurveda vs. Chinese Medicine](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-ayurveda-vs-chinese-medicine.html): Compares the visit, home practice, training, safety and progress measurement rather than declaring one system superior.
- [Traditional Persian Medicine Benefits](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/traditional-persian-medicine-benefits.html): English evidence-aware guide to the historical framework, potential practical benefits, research limits, safety and VEDICA's integration of Persian medicine with Ayurveda.
- [Ceylon vs. Cassia Cinnamon](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-cinnamon.html): Covers botanical labeling, coumarin, food use versus concentrated products and evidence limitations.
- [Sourdough and Digestion in Ayurveda](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-sourdough.html): Distinguishes sourdough evidence from marketing, explains product comparison and states that wheat or spelt sourdough is not safe for celiac disease.
- [Basmati Rice in Ayurveda](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-basmati.html): Separates traditional use from glycemic evidence and evaluates portion, cooking and meal context.
- [Bread, Scalded Dough, and Kombucha](https://vedica-ayurveda.co.il/blog-bread.html): Explains how the three processes differ and why kombucha in baked bread does not guarantee live probiotics.

## Editorial And Medical Boundaries

- The updated articles visibly identify Inbal Hanasab as author and show publication and modification dates.
- Traditional Ayurvedic concepts are labeled as traditional frameworks rather than presented as established biomedical facts.
- Medical and nutrition claims link to relevant sources from NCCIH, NIDDK, NHLBI, ASRM or peer-reviewed PubMed records.
- The articles do not promise cure, fertility outcomes or guaranteed digestive improvement, and they identify symptoms that require medical assessment.
